en.extract:
The yellow-bellied whistler (Pachycephala philippinensis), or Philippine whistler, is a species of bird in the family Pachycephalidae that is endemic to the Philippines. Its natural habitats are tropical moist lowland forest and  the lower reaches tropical moist montane forest.


Description and taxonomy


Subspecies
Seven subspecies are recognized:

P. p. fallax – (McGregor, 1904): Originally described as a separate species. Found on Calayan Island (northern Philippines)
P. p. illex – (McGregor, 1907): Originally described as a separate species. Found on Camiguin Norte Island (northern Philippines)
P. p. philippinensis – (Walden, 1872): Found on Luzon and Catanduanes (northern Philippines)
P. p. siquijorensis – Rand & Rabor, 1957: Found on Siquijor (south-central Philippines)
P. p. apoensis – (Mearns, 1905): Originally described as a separate species. Found in east-central and southern Philippines
P. p. basilanica – (Mearns, 1909): Found in Basilan (southwestern Philippines)
P. p. boholensis – Parkes, 1966: Found in Bohol (south-central Philippines)


Ecology and behavior
Forages in the understory for insects and often joins mixed-species flocks that include Blue-headed fantail, Golden-crowned babbler, Lemon-throated leaf warbler, Black-crowned babbler Little pied flycatcher, Turquoise flycatcher, Negros leaf warbler and other small birds. Adults with enlarged gonads from January to May, nestlings in Apr and nest and eggs in May. Nest is an open cup made out of fine roots and leaves placed 3 meters above understorey.


Habitat and conservation status
This species habitat is primary and secondary forest up to 1,220 metres (4,000 ft) above sea level.
IUCN has assessed this bird as least-concern species despite deforestation in the Philippines continues throughout the country due to slash and burn farming, mining, illegal logging and habitat conversion. 
It is found in multiple protected areas such as Bicol Natural Park, Pasonanca Natural Park, Rajah Sikatuna Protected Landscape,  Samar Island Natural Park but like all areas in the Philippines, protection is lax and deforestation continues despite this protection on paper.

The '''yellow-bellied whistler''' ('''''Pachycephala philippinensis'''''), or '''Philippine whistler''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Pachycephalidae]] that is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the Philippines. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest|tropical moist lowland forest]] and  the lower reaches tropical moist [[montane forest]].

== Description and taxonomy ==

=== Subspecies ===
Seven [[subspecies]] are recognized:<ref>{{Cite web |title=Whiteheads, sitellas & whistlers « IOC World Bird List |url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/whistlers/ |access-date=2017-02-01 |website=www.worldbirdnames.org |language=en-US}}</ref>

*''P. p. fallax'' – <small>([[Richard Crittenden McGregor|McGregor]], 1904)</small>: Originally described as a separate species. Found on [[Calayan Island]] (northern Philippines)
* ''P. p. illex'' – <small>(McGregor, 1907)</small>: Originally described as a separate species. Found on [[Camiguin|Camiguin Norte Island]] (northern Philippines)
* ''P. p. philippinensis'' – <small>([[Arthur Hay, 9th Marquess of Tweeddale|Walden]], 1872)</small>: Found on [[Luzon]] and [[Catanduanes]] (northern Philippines)
* ''P. p. siquijorensis'' – <small>[[Austin L. Rand|Rand]] & [[Dioscoro S. Rabor|Rabor]], 1957</small>: Found on [[Siquijor]] (south-central Philippines)
* ''P. p. apoensis'' – <small>([[Edgar Alexander Mearns|Mearns]], 1905)</small>: Originally described as a separate species. Found in east-central and southern Philippines
* ''P. p. basilanica'' – <small>(Mearns, 1909)</small>: Found in [[Basilan]] (southwestern Philippines)
* ''P. p. boholensis'' – <small>[[Kenneth Carroll Parkes|Parkes]], 1966</small>: Found in [[Bohol]] (south-central Philippines)

== Ecology and behavior ==
Forages in the understory for insects and often joins mixed-species flocks that include [[Blue-headed fantail]], [[Golden-crowned babbler]], [[Lemon-throated leaf warbler]], [[Calabarzon babbler|Black-crowned babbler]] [[Little pied flycatcher]], [[Turquoise flycatcher]], [[Negros leaf warbler]] and other small birds. Adults with enlarged gonads from January to May, nestlings in Apr and nest and eggs in May. Nest is an open cup made out of fine roots and leaves placed 3 meters above understorey.<ref>{{Cite taxon|BOW|last=Boles |first=Walter |date=2020 |title=Yellow-bellied Whistler (''Pachycephala philippinensis'')|version=1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/yebwhi1/1.0/introduction |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.yebwhi1.01 |issn=2771-3105}}</ref>

== Habitat and conservation status ==
This species habitat is primary and secondary forest up to {{convert|1220|m|-1}} above sea level.

IUCN has assessed this bird as [[least-concern species]] despite [[Deforestation in the Philippines|deforestation]] in the Philippines continues throughout the country due to slash and burn farming, mining, illegal logging and habitat conversion. 

It is found in multiple protected areas such as [[Bicol Natural Park]], [[Pasonanca Natural Park]], [[Rajah Sikatuna Protected Landscape]],  [[Samar Island Natural Park]] but like all areas in the Philippines, protection is lax and deforestation continues despite this protection on paper.<ref name="iucn"/>

zh.extract:
黄腹啸鹟（学名：Pachycephala philippinensis），是啸鹟科啸鹟属的一种，为菲律宾的特有种。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
黄腹啸鹟的平均体重约为22.3克。栖息地包括亚热带或热带的湿润低地林和亚热带或热带的湿润山地林。


参考文献
